action.thought_summary: The initial requirement is clear but lacks detail on sources, scope, and user interaction. Starting with a content/data probe to understand what 'beauty news' encompasses and where it comes from.
action.question: What sources should the website automatically collect beauty news from — for example, specific magazines, blogs, social media platforms, or RSS feeds? And what types of beauty topics are in scope (e.g., skincare, makeup, haircare, wellness)?

action.thought_summary: The user mentioned 'a specified beauty channel' but was uncertain about specific sources. I need to clarify what they mean by this — is it a single source, a configurable list, or something else? This is a clarification question about the collection mechanism.
action.question: When you say 'a specified beauty channel,' do you mean the website should pull news from one single source that you designate (like a specific website URL or RSS feed), or should it support multiple channels that you can add and manage over time?

action.thought_summary: User wants auto-collection from a specified beauty channel but hasn't defined channel management. Need to probe whether they need to modify/update the source after setup, which is an interaction/workflow question. Turn 3 should cover interaction behavior.
action.question: Once you've designated a beauty channel as the source, should you be able to change or update that source later through the website's settings, or is it a one-time setup that stays fixed?

action.thought_summary: User is uncertain about channel management details. Pivoting to content/data needs - what article information should be displayed and how articles should be organized on the website.
action.question: When the website displays the collected beauty articles, what information should be shown for each article — for example, just the title and link, or also the publication date, source name, summary text, images, or the full article content?

action.thought_summary: User has been vague on source management, display details, and flexibility. At turn 5, should cover content/data needs. Will ask a concrete question about article organization/filtering to uncover implicit content requirements.
action.question: Should users be able to search or filter the collected beauty articles — for example, by keyword, date range, or beauty category — or is a simple chronological list of all articles sufficient?

action.thought_summary: User has been vague on multiple fronts. Turn 6 should probe style/UI per coverage schedule. Ask a concrete, visual-oriented question about how the website should look and feel to help the user articulate their preferences.
action.question: When you picture the website in your mind, do you see it looking more like a news feed with articles listed vertically (similar to a blog), or more like a magazine-style layout with featured images and grid sections, or something minimal like a simple list of headlines?

action.thought_summary: The user has been unsure about most aspects but revealed concrete style preferences (amber background, chocolate components). I should probe a specific interaction detail that hasn't been covered yet - the collection frequency/automation behavior - since this is core to the 'automatic collection' requirement.

action.thought_summary: The user has provided style preferences (amber background, chocolate components) but remains undecided on many functional aspects. I should probe a concrete interaction scenario to uncover implicit requirements — specifically what users can do with articles after they're displayed, which may reveal needs like bookmarking, sharing, or clicking through to originals.

action.thought_summary: The user has been largely undecided on many aspects. I've covered interaction, content, and style basics. A concrete question about handling many articles (pagination vs. infinite scroll) could reveal an implicit interaction requirement and help define the display behavior more concretely.
action.question: As articles accumulate over time, should the website show a fixed number of articles per page with pagination controls (like 'Next' and 'Previous' buttons), or should it use infinite scroll where new articles load as you scroll down, or display only the most recent articles (e.g., the latest 20 or 50)?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently uncertain about many aspects. I've covered interaction, content/data, and style categories. The user revealed one concrete preference: amber background with chocolate-colored components. I should try a more specific, scenario-based question to uncover implicit requirements. Let me ask about the target audience and whether the site needs user accounts or is publicly accessible, as this affects many design decisions.

action.thought_summary: The user has revealed several concrete requirements: customizing website title/keywords/description, Sina SAE platform option, and amber background with chocolate components. action.thought_summary: The user has revealed requirements for automatic collection from a beauty channel, amber background with chocolate components, customizable title/keywords/description, and dual SAE/standard deployment. This is a content/data and interaction gap worth exploring.

And if using scheduled tasks, should the cron job configuration be part of the website settings so you can control it?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many aspects but has revealed concrete preferences: amber background, chocolate components, customizable title/keywords/description, and dual SAE/standard deployment. I've covered interaction, content, and style broadly. A practical implicit requirement to probe is mobile responsiveness, as most websites need it but users rarely mention it upfront.

action.thought_summary: After 18 turns, the user has consistently indicated uncertainty on most detailed aspects. Key confirmed requirements: auto-collect from a specified beauty channel, customizable title/keywords/description, dual SAE/standard deployment with free switching, amber background with chocolate components. Most other aspects (pagination, auth, error handling, persistence, source format, etc.) remain undefined. Further probing is unlikely to yield new concrete requirements. Time to finish.
action.finish_summary: Elicited requirements for a beauty news website: (1) Automatically collect and display articles from a specified beauty channel (source format and collection mechanism undefined); (2) Customizable website title, keywords, and description (configuration method and keyword purpose undefined); (3) Support both Sina SAE deployment and standard deployment, with the ability to switch freely between environments; (4) Visual style: amber page background with chocolate-colored components.
